mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year historical backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which expands corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to yield a positive return frequency above 52% over rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which improves near-term earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production changes coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 60% of observations during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation exceeds 2.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, real interest rates turn deeply negative, prompting market pricing of imminent monetary tightening and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns with a frequency exceeding 50% when both threshold conditions are met over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls indicate economic expansion and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.01 or positive return frequency drops below 48% during periods of consistent payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate below 1.5% signals economic stagnation and weak aggregate demand, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of quarters where annualized GDP growth falls below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized labor and output expansion, reinforcing near-term earnings momentum and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 5-day returns in greater than 50% of occurrences over a rolling 3-year evaluation window.
